headline: Heat breaks to 94°F — tip canes and feed tomatoes.
weather_notes: Run both zones this morning — 3 days dry at 94°F today, rising to 97°F Thursday with no reliable rain until Friday.
Do all garden work before 8 AM today through Friday — 94–98°F daily highs close every afternoon outdoor window.
Apply fish emulsion to e1–e4 and w1 by Thursday morning — Friday's 40% storm chance could wash off same-day applications.
Watch Friday's storm forecast before scheduling next week's zone run — a real rain event could replace one irrigation cycle.

surfaced_tasks.title: Tip new blackberry primocanes if 3–4 ft tall
surfaced_tasks.reason: 5 days overdue — today's 94°F is the first bearable morning window after last week's 100–108°F heat wave.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed e1–e4 tomatoes with fish emulsion
suggested_tasks.description: 25-day transplants have no logged fertilizer — apply by Thursday morning before Friday's 40% storm chance could wash off the application.

suggested_tasks.title: Feed w1 strawberries with berry food
suggested_tasks.description: 42 days since the Jun 3 feed — apply Thursday morning before Friday's 40% storm narrows the clean application window.
